# Grant — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Grant | **Tracker:** Eh_Grant | **Role:** IGL / Flex | **Email:** grantgreenfelder@gmail.com

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** The team's IGL and best gun. Grant leads with reads, wins the isolated duels nobody else takes, and holds composure when the round is on the line.

**Tendencies:** Plays best when in full control of his own actions. Doesn't need frag assistance. Comfortable in isolated, high-pressure moments. Highest clutch win% on the team — composure is a consistent trait, not a season fluke.

**Role fit:** IGL / Flex

**Solo vs. Group:** #1 priority for solo roles in any formation.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Best player on the team** by K/D and clutch metrics across tracked seasons. No structural weaknesses.
- **Chalet** is a recurring team-wide problem — not Grant-specific. Don't assign individual blame.
- Low HS% relative to teammates (mid-30s%) is consistent with his calm, cerebral aim style — not a weakness to correct.
- IGL role is appropriate and earns the stack its best outcomes. Don't pull him into rigid formed roles that limit his reads.

# Grant — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-26 | 178M sample_

---

## Your Role

IGL and primary fragger. You call the exec, you win the solo duel when it matters. At 178M the season picture is well-established — DEF is locked in and consistent, ATK pool management and map selection are the ongoing levers to work on. The team plays around your reads, so the main coaching focus is on making sure op and map choices reflect what's actually converting.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — This side is locked in.**
Your top four DEF ops are all producing at significant volume:
- Mute — 188r | 53.7% | 1.21 ✅ (primary anchor — consistent at high volume)
- Kapkan — 64r | 56.2% | 1.93 ✅ (trap setups are working — elite K/D)
- Thorn — 61r | 60.7% | 1.74 ⭐ (strong WR and K/D sustained across the season)
- Castle — 62r | 59.7% | 1.28 ⭐ (reliable second anchor)

That's 375 rounds across four ops all at 53%+ WR. The DEF formula is solid. Maestro (19r / 68.4%) is a bright spot in secondary rounds too — worth keeping in the pool on sites where Evil Eye denial is valuable.

**ATK — Thatcher is still the right primary.**
Thatcher — 81r | 44.4% | 1.26. Hard breach support at meaningful volume. Hibana (51r / 49.0%) is the nearest secondary and performing around baseline. Together these two cover the hard-breach role reliably.

**Maps — Border is your strongest personal map.**
- Border — 22M | 68.2% ⭐ (your best result at real volume)
- Chalet — 15M | 60.0%
- Villa — 14M | 50.0%
- Kafe Dostoyevsky — 12M | 50.0%

Border at 68.2% over 22 matches is your clearest map strength — worth protecting in ban discussions. Chalet at 60.0% is a solid second.

---

## Areas to Explore

**ATK — Sledge and Lion are 60r+ each at below-40% WR.**
- Sledge — 60r | 38.3% ⚠️
- Lion — 51r | 39.2% ⚠️

Both have now built full-season volume and both are sitting well below 40% WR. At 178M, this is a confirmed pattern rather than variance. Worth asking whether these ops should continue at their current volume, or whether rounds could shift toward Hibana or Amaru (40r / 42.5%) and see if the ATK WR recovers. You don't have to drop them — but the sustained signal is worth acting on.

**ATK — A few utility picks are producing below 35% WR.**
- Gridlock — 22r | 27.3% ⚠️
- Solid Snake — 17r | 35.3% ⚠️
- Blitz — 20r | 25.0% ⚠️

These have accumulated meaningful rounds at very low WR. If they're experimental or situational, that context makes sense. If they're going in as serious picks, the results suggest they're not working in their current usage.

**Maps — Clubhouse has softened and Coastline remains below baseline.**
- Clubhouse — 17M | 52.9% (was 66.7% at 12M — has drifted toward even)
- Coastline — 20M | 40.0% (persistent — 15M sample last time was also 40.0%)
- Lair — 5M | 20.0% (small sample but clear avoid signal)

Coastline is the most notable map concern — 20 matches at 40.0% is a confirmed result. Worth flagging for the team ban discussion as a map to avoid rather than push through.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Try pulling back Sledge and Lion volume.**
Both are above 50 rounds at below-40% WR — enough evidence to experiment with. Shift some of those rounds to Hibana and Amaru for a stretch and track whether the ATK WR moves. This doesn't have to be permanent — just a trial to see if the pattern changes.

**2. Coastline belongs in the ban discussion.**
20 matches at 40.0% WR and it's been consistent all season. If the team is regularly playing Coastline and losing there, it's worth pushing against it more actively at ban time. Emerald Plains (5M / 0.0%) and Lair (5M / 20.0%) are also maps to avoid when you have input.

**3. DEF is your anchor — trust the formula.**
375 rounds across Mute/Kapkan/Thorn/Castle all at 53%+. The DEF identity is working. The main coaching work this season is on the ATK side — use the DEF consistency as the foundation and keep refining the ATK op distribution.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your Y11S1.md file._

teachingPriorities.teaching: Pre-aim where the head will be before you arrive — mindset, not mechanics
After a kill, push in immediately — the space only exists if you claim it before they rotate
Pre-fire from frame edge calibrated by distance; wide swing the instant you see any part of them; swing far from the wall
Don't re-peek the same height/position — vary height, width, or approach route each time
Trade call: "[enemy location], pushing, trade me" — convert pushes into guaranteed follow-through

mainStack.teachingPattern: Grant's strongest area is likely game-sense/decision-making; crosshair placement (HS% 46.1%) and post-kill space-taking are the mechanical gaps. Peeking mechanics tips are a ceiling upgrade once crosshair placement is habitual.

# Peej — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-26 | 99M sample_

---

## Your Role

Entry fragger and DEF anchor. Your DEF side is one of the strongest individual performances on the roster — Caveira at 110 rounds and 59.1% WR is confirmed at high volume. The main coaching story this season is ATK: both primary picks (Ash and Twitch) are sitting at 39.7% WR at significant volume, while Brava is performing much better with less play time. The data is making a clear case to shift volume toward Brava as the ATK anchor.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — Caveira is confirmed at elite volume.**
- Caveira — 110r | 59.1% | 1.59 ✅ (dominant — high volume, consistently above baseline)
- Thorn — 33r | 63.6% | 1.64 ⭐ (best WR in your DEF pool — outstanding)
- Azami — 18r | 66.7% | 1.31 ⭐ (strong results in its rounds)
- Valkyrie — 56r | 57.1% | 1.39 ✅ (reliable second anchor)

This DEF pool is elite. Caveira at 110 rounds and 59.1% is one of the strongest op performances on the full roster this season. Thorn at 63.6% is exceptional — worth giving it more rounds alongside Caveira. Azami's numbers are a bright spot worth noting.

**ATK — Brava is your most efficient pick.**
Brava — 34r | 55.9% | 1.55 ✅. Still sitting 16 points above Ash and Twitch. The Kludge Virus disruption model complements aggressive entry well and appears to be converting rounds in a way the other primary picks aren't.

**Maps — Border and Villa are your dominant maps.**
- Border — 12M | 83.3% ⭐ (elite result at real volume)
- Villa — 7M | 71.4% ⭐
- Bank — 12M | 58.3%
- Nighthaven Labs — 10M | 50.0%

# Hound — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Hound | **Tracker:** Eh_Hound | **Role:** Support | **Email:** shadowhound6193@gmail.com

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** The backbone of the stack's coordinated pushes. Hound wins rounds through utility, callouts, and enabling the team's fraggers — one of the most consistently active players on the roster.

**Tendencies:** Group player. Passive support. Strong with utility operators. Rarely out of position — disciplined. Sub-1.0 K/D is a career pattern but team win rates stay positive because of his enabling role.

**Role fit:** Support Anchor

**Solo vs. Group:** Avoid solo roles. Weakest gun on the team — solo entry puts him in duels he is not built to win. Keep him in a group where utility is the contribution.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Never assign solo entry.** This is a standing instruction across all seasons — profile confirmed by data.
- **DEF is where he contributes most individual value.** Attack is enabling work; defense is where individual efficiency shows.
- **Castle as primary DEF anchor** is a recurring pattern across seasons — worth investigating whether this is intentional coordination strategy or just comfort. If intentional, lean into barricade callout protocols.
- **Frost** consistently outperforms Castle on individual metrics — worth asking whether Frost should lead in volume.
- Attack K/Ds are low by design — he must always be grouped with a fragger who does the killing.
- **ATK role reminder:** Hound provides the breach/gadget clearance. Someone else gets the kills. This is working — don't break it.

mainStack.coachingContent:
# Hound — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 (91M)_

---

## Your Role

Team support and group anchor. On ATK, your value is in the group execute — enabling the push, not winning solo duels. On DEF, you hold site with utility and coordination. At 91M, the Y11S1 picture is showing a pattern on ATK worth paying attention to: most of the high-volume ops are trending below 40% WR, while some of the lower-volume picks are producing noticeably better.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — Azami and Frost are producing your best results.**
- Azami — 29r | 62.1% | 1.15 ⭐ (best WR in your DEF pool with meaningful volume)
- Frost — 26r | 57.7% | 1.06 ✅ (outperforms Castle on WR and K/D)
- Bandit — 39r | 53.8% ✅ (reliable secondary)
- Maestro — 12r | 66.7% | 1.25 ⭐ (small sample, but a strong positive signal)

Your DEF ceiling is genuinely high when you're on these ops. They're winning rounds at a rate that Castle and Mute aren't matching right now.

**ATK — Rauora and Gridlock are your best-performing picks.**
- Rauora — 15r | 53.3% | 0.73 ✅
- Gridlock — 11r | 72.7% | 4.00 ⭐ (very small sample — treat as directional only)
- Buck — 7r | 57.1% | 1.40 (small but positive)

These three fit your support role well — area denial, flank coverage, group enable. They're also outperforming everything above them in your op list by rounds. That's worth noticing.

**Maps — Border, Clubhouse, and Bank are your bright spots.**
Border (14M / 57.1%), Clubhouse (8M / 62.5%), Bank (6M / 66.7%), Coastline (11M / 54.5%). These four are your best options right now for ban-phase protection.

---

## Areas to Watch

**ATK: Most of the high-volume ops are trending below 40% WR.**

| Op | Rounds | WR | Note |
|----|--------|----|------|
| Thermite | 40r | 35.0% | ⚠️ |
| Osa | 38r | 36.8% | ⚠️ |
| Grim | 30r | 43.3% | — |
| Thatcher | 29r | 41.4% | ⚠️ |
| Nomad | 28r | 32.1% | ⚠️ |
| Fuze | 23r | 39.1% | ⚠️ |

Five of the top six by round count are flagged. That could mean a lot of things — tough sites, matchup variance, a team coordination pattern on certain ops — so it's worth thinking about rather than assuming it's op selection alone. That said, Rauora (53.3%) and Gridlock (72.7%) are both outperforming this whole group in smaller samples, and both fit your support role just as well. It's at least worth trying to shift some volume toward them and seeing if the round results feel different.

**DEF: Castle is leading your DEF by rounds but sits below your other options on WR and K/D.**
Castle — 53r | 49.1% | 0.79. Azami (62.1%) and Frost (57.7%) are both outperforming it by a meaningful margin, and they have enough rounds now to say that's a real pattern. If Castle is your default pick on most sites out of habit, it might be worth asking whether Azami or Frost would be a better fit before the round starts — especially on sites where you don't specifically need the barricade utility that Castle brings.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Try giving Rauora more ATK volume.**
It's at 15 rounds and 53.3% WR — the best-performing meaningful ATK pick you have. It fits your support identity naturally (area denial, flank coverage) and the early results are encouraging. See if you can get it up to 30+ rounds and let a larger sample tell the story.

**2. Question Osa before each round.**
38 rounds at 36.8% is a pattern worth examining. That said — if Osa is being used for specific utility (shield for plant cover, corner peek control) and the team is capitalizing on it, the WR might not tell the full story. The question to ask yourself before going Osa: does the team have a plan to use what Osa provides? If not, Rauora or Grim might give better return.

**3. DEF: Try Azami or Frost as your default before defaulting to Castle.**
Castle has a clear role — barricade utility on specific sites — but if it's becoming the automatic pick on most rounds, it might be worth swapping the default. Try going Azami or Frost first and using Castle deliberately on the sites where the walls matter. The WR difference (62% vs. 49%) is meaningful enough to experiment.

**4. Stay grouped — this is still the standing instruction.**
Your gun is not the team's fragger. Your value comes from the group execute — when you're running with Smigs and Grant, the team's coordination is at its strongest. If a push opportunity comes up and it's a solo run, hold it and wait for the group to be ready.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

The question to ask yourself before going Osa: does the team have a plan to use what Osa provides? If not, Rauora or Grim might give better return.

**3. DEF: Try Azami or Frost as your default before defaulting to Castle.**
Castle has a clear role — barricade utility on specific sites — but if it's becoming the automatic pick on most rounds, it might be worth swapping the default. Try going Azami or Frost first and using Castle deliberately on the sites where the walls matter. The WR difference (62% vs. 49%) is meaningful enough to experiment.

**4. Stay grouped — this is still the standing instruction.**
Your gun is not the team's fragger. Your value comes from the group execute — when you're running with Smigs and Grant, the team's coordination is at its strongest. If a push opportunity comes up and it's a solo run, hold it and wait for the group to be ready.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

**Strengths:** The most reliable support player on the roster. Smigs brings a team-first mentality to every round — utility clearing, hard breach, and group entry without needing the frag lead.

**Solo vs. Group:** Avoid solo entry pushes. Keep Smigs in a group.

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Win rate is the signal, not K/D.** His support-first mentality produces wins consistently. Don't coach him toward fragging — it'll break what works.
- **Capitão** was a Y10S4 standout — 60.9% WR in 23r, the highest ATK win rate in his pool that season. Not yet in his all-time ATK top 5 (Thatcher/Thermite/Nomad/Zofia/Ace), so the sample is still growing. Worth giving more volume in Y11S1 to confirm if this is a trend.
- **Skyscraper** was a personal weakness in Y10S4 (40.0% WR, 0.52 K/D in 5 matches) but all-time he sits at 50.0% on Skyscraper — so this is season-specific, not a career pattern. Do not treat as a permanent avoidance map.
- **Stack play is where he adds the most value.** His WR in stack vs. solo queue is meaningfully different — the more he plays with the team, the better the outcomes.
- Zone-control DEF anchor (Kaid/Aruni/Castle) is a stable identity across seasons.

mainStack.coachingContent:
# Smigs — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-26 | 60M sample_

---

## Your Role

Utility anchor and hard-breach core. On ATK, Thermite is your confirmed primary and it's doing its job. On DEF, you have one of the deepest op pools on the roster — Nomad, Mute, Bandit, Thorn, and Castle are all posting strong numbers. The question this season is whether Kaid is getting more rounds than it should, given that it's your primary by volume but sitting below several ops that are outperforming it with less play time.

---

## What's Working

**ATK — Thermite is confirmed.**
Thermite — 77r | 46.8% | 1.35. Solid primary at close to 47% WR with real volume. Hibana (20r / 35.0%) is the secondary hard-breach option, but the WR suggests it may be working into harder sites or sub-optimal setups — worth being selective about when Hibana goes in versus defaulting to Thermite.

**DEF — The backup pool is performing above the primary.**
The standout numbers are in the ops getting fewer rounds:
- Nomad — 12r | 75.0% ⭐
- Mute — 12r | 66.7% ⭐
- Castle — 8r | 62.5% ⭐
- Thorn — 19r | 57.9% ✅
- Bandit — 19r | 57.9% ✅
- Aruni — 30r | 50.0%

Five ops at 57%+. That's a genuinely strong DEF pool — the question is just whether round distribution reflects it.

**Maps — Clubhouse is still your clearest team strength.**
Clubhouse (5M / 80.0%) and Oregon (3M / 66.7%) are the most positive maps with enough rounds to note. Sample sizes are still modest at 60M spread across 11 maps — directional signals rather than confirmed reads. Worth tracking into next pull.

---

## Areas to Explore

**DEF: Kaid is your primary op at 41r, but it's posting 43.9% WR.**
Kaid — 41r | 43.9% | 1.19. Kaid is getting the most DEF rounds while sitting below 45% WR, while Nomad (75.0%), Mute (66.7%), Castle (62.5%), Thorn (57.9%), and Bandit (57.9%) are all posting significantly stronger results with less volume. That doesn't mean Kaid is wrong on every site — the Rtila Claw's gadget denial is valuable in the right setups — but it's worth asking before a DEF round whether Kaid is the right call for that specific site, or whether Mute, Bandit, or Thorn would convert more reliably.

**ATK: Hibana's secondary breach role may need revisiting.**
Hibana — 20r | 35.0% ⚠️. At 35% WR with 20 rounds, the gap from Thermite is noticeable. Could be site difficulty on hard-breach spots, or Hibana being queued on sites where Thermite would work better. Worth being selective — Thermite at 46.8% is the clear driver here.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Think about DEF op selection before each round — Kaid vs. the pool.**
The volume and the results aren't lining up on Kaid right now. Before a DEF round, the quick mental check: does this site need Kaid's Rtila Claw specifically (gadget denial + electrification), or would Mute, Bandit, or Thorn win this round more reliably? The DEF pool is strong — make sure round allocation reflects what the site calls for.

**2. Aruni is a better anchor than Kaid on most sites.**
Aruni (30r / 50.0%) is sitting above Kaid's WR and has real sample. On sites without a strong electrical surface denial need, Aruni's Prisma laser grid tends to offer more flexible defensive utility. Worth making Aruni the default when Kaid doesn't have a specific reason to go in.

**3. Thermite is confirmed — ATK identity is set.**
77 rounds at 46.8% WR with 1.35 K/D is a solid primary. Focus on being selective about when Hibana goes in as secondary — reserve it for sites where Thermite genuinely can't do the job, rather than rotating to it by default.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your Y11S1.md file._

# Sarge — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Sarge | **Tracker:** Eh_SiegeyBodeez | **Role:** Entry / Fragger

⚠️ **Callsign note:** Some old script files and earlier KB docs swap Smigs/Sarge. Authoritative mapping: **Sarge = Eh_SiegeyBodeez**, Smigs = Eh_tooten. Trust tracker usernames over callsign labels in any document with ambiguity.

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** A two-way player whose real identity lives on defense. Vigil is his career anchor, and on attack he's most effective leading the three-man group rather than working solo.

**Tendencies:** Coordination similar to Grant but less composed under pressure. Excels at getting early man advantage or map control. Comfortable leading a sub-group. **Defense is his dominant side** — this is a consistent career pattern, not just a season anomaly.

**Role fit:** Sub-IGL / Anchor

**Solo vs. Group:** Most valuable leading a 2–3 man group. Solo roles should go to Grant or Peej first (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21).

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Role/op mismatch is the standing coaching priority.** Called "Entry/Fragger" but his Y10S4 ATK top 5 (Thermite/Buck/IQ/Sledge/Hibana) posted sub-1.0 K/D on every operator this season. Note: all-time, three of his top 5 ATK ops have hit 1.0+ K/D (Ace 1.15, Thatcher 1.02, Sledge 1.02) — so the issue is Y10S4 op selection, not solely mechanics. Either evolve toward actual entry ops (Ash, Zofia, Iana) or update the role label.
- **Defense is his real identity.** Vigil is the career DEF anchor — all-time #1 DEF op (716r / 57.8% / 1.45 K/D, Y8S1+). Thunderbird is a strong Y10S4 addition (115r this season) but does not appear in his all-time top 5 DEF ops (Vigil/Mute/Bandit/Doc/Rook) — treat it as emerging, not career-proven. In stack play, his DEF rounds are the priority.
- **Rank gap** (historically Platinum while the rest of the stack sits at Emerald) needs a direct conversation. Solo queue vs. stack performance should be investigated.
- In stack formation: assign Sarge to lead the 3-man group on attack while Grant/Peej take solo roles (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21). His coordination capability makes him the best choice for sub-group IGL.
- **Outback, Border, Villa** are recurring strong maps — protect these in ban phase.
- **Chalet** is a consistent team-wide problem, not Sarge-specific.

mainStack.coachingContent:
# Sarge — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 (117M)_

---

## Your Role

Sub-IGL and roam threat. On DEF, Vigil roaming is your identity — your K/D on Vigil is the highest individual defensive K/D on the main stack. On ATK, you lead the 3-man group and have built a real Zofia identity over 163 rounds this season. The Zofia transition worked. The area worth digging into now is Jackal, which is carrying a lot of rounds at lower WR.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — Vigil and Azami are your 1–2.**
- Vigil — 105r | 56.2% | 1.39 ✅ (roaming identity, elite K/D)
- Azami — 62r | 59.7% | 1.24 ✅ (strong anchor complement)
- Melusi — 15r | 60.0% ⭐ (smaller sample, but a positive signal worth watching)

167 rounds across your top two ops at 57%+ WR. DEF is your dominant side. When you roam on Vigil, a simple heads-up to the anchors before the round ("going X flank, back in 90") helps the rest of the team play their anchor without guessing your position.

**ATK — Zofia identity is confirmed.**
163r / 43.6% / 1.20 K/D — a full season's worth of volume and K/D solidly above 1.0. That's a meaningful step up from Y10S4 where every ATK op was sub-1.0 K/D. The transition landed. Thatcher (16r / 56.2% ✅) and Iana (13r / 61.5% ⭐) are both producing well in secondary rounds — worth letting those grow.

**Maps — Four clear strengths.**
Clubhouse (11M / 72.7%), Coastline (9M / 66.7%), Border (14M / 64.3%), Chalet (10M / 60.0%). All above 60% WR with solid samples. These four are worth protecting in the ban phase when you have a say.

---

## Areas to Watch

**ATK: Jackal is carrying 62 rounds at 32.3% WR — worth a closer look.**
Jackal — 62r | 32.3% | 1.18 ⚠️

That's your second-most-played ATK op this season, and WR is sitting at 32.3%. There are a few possible explanations — it may be getting queued in tough matchups, or the drone-track intel isn't translating to round wins in team play, or it's just running in a rough stretch. But at 62 rounds the trend is consistent enough to be worth questioning. It might be worth pulling Jackal back and giving those rounds to Zofia or Thatcher for a stretch, then seeing if the overall ATK WR shifts. If the WR recovers, that's a useful signal about where those rounds are best spent.

**Maps — Oregon and Consulate are trending poorly at meaningful sample sizes.**
- Oregon — 14M | 28.6% (ATK 35.0%, DEF 42.9% — both sides struggling)
- Consulate — 11M | 27.3% (ATK side at 40.0% is where it's slipping)

These could be rough matchup clusters or specific site problems — hard to know from WR alone. But the sample sizes are real enough to flag. If the team is looking for ban targets and you're drawing Oregon or Consulate often, it's worth raising. Oregon in particular has both sides below 43%.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Experiment with pulling Jackal back.**
It's not a certainty that Jackal is the problem — but 62 rounds at 32.3% is enough of a pattern to test against. Try a session or two leaning on Zofia primary + Thatcher secondary and track whether the ATK results feel different. If the team's breach timing improves when Jackal isn't in the loop, that's a real answer. If results stay flat, it may be something else entirely.

**2. Oregon might be worth flagging in your ban discussions.**
With 14 matches at 28.6%, it's your lowest-WR map with a real sample. You know this map better than the stats do — if there are specific sites or setups that are genuinely tough for how you play, it's worth making the case to the group. Don't just absorb the map because nobody said anything.

**3. Vigil roam — communicate the plan.**
Your Vigil K/D is elite, but roaming value multiplies when the anchors know where you are. A quick call before round start about where you're going and when to expect contact helps the team play with you instead of reacting to you. It doesn't take long and the round flow gets cleaner.

**4. Protect your four map strengths in ban phase.**
Clubhouse (72.7%), Coastline (66.7%), Border (64.3%), Chalet (60.0%) are your best four this season. When the team is negotiating ban phase, make the case for keeping these. They replaced the older Outback/Villa priority that's now less relevant.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Slug — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-26 | 107M sample_

---

## Your Role

Second IGL and B Team's most experienced solo-queue anchor. Your DEF identity is firmly established — Valkyrie and Solis form one of the strongest DEF cores on the roster, and the intel-read playstyle suits your independent decision-making well. On ATK, Jackal has emerged as your primary pick, but the volume distribution across ATK ops is worth examining this season. The map picture at 107M is now reliable — Oregon and Villa are high-volume maps that are trending below 50%, which is worth flagging in ban discussions.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — Valkyrie/Solis core is locked in.**
- Valkyrie — 91r | 62.6% | 1.18 ⭐ (your clear anchor — strong at primary volume)
- Solis — 49r | 61.2% | 1.49 ⭐ (excellent second pick — intel + combat efficient)
- Smoke — 46r | 52.2% ✅ (solid third option)
- Castle — 13r | 61.5% ⭐ (strong in situational rounds)

The Valkyrie/Solis pairing is a genuinely strong DEF core — both operators reward the read-heavy, intel-first approach you play. Keep building on it.

**ATK — Zero and Capitao are your highest-performing picks.**
Zero is no longer in the top tier by rounds, but Chalet (6M / 66.7%) and the broader pattern suggest the intel-gathering approach carries over. Capitao at meaningful volume has been a strong ATK secondary. Worth leaning into whichever of these is producing best in current volume.

**Maps — Border, Coastline, and Chalet are your clearest strengths.**
- Chalet — 6M | 66.7% ⭐
- Border — 10M | 60.0%
- Coastline — 10M | 50.0% (even with good K/D)
- Consulate — 7M | 57.1%

Border and Chalet are the maps worth protecting at ban time. Coastline is breaking even at meaningful volume.

---

## Areas to Explore

**ATK — Hibana is your secondary ATK op at 34r, but it's posting 38.2% WR.**
Hibana — 34r | 38.2% ⚠️. Hard breach on ATK is a useful role, but at 38.2% WR with 34 rounds the current setup isn't converting well. Could be site matchup, timing, or coordination factors — but the gap from the DEF side is wide enough that it's worth asking whether Hibana rounds could shift toward stronger-performing ATK picks.

**Maps — Oregon and Villa are your two highest-volume maps and both are below 45% WR.**
- Oregon — 14M | 42.9% (your highest-volume map)
- Villa — 13M | 38.5% (second-highest volume)

Both maps are seeing a lot of rounds and both are trending below 45%. Oregon in particular — 14 matches at 42.9% is a real signal, not noise. Both are worth flagging in team ban discussions when the lineup has input. Nighthaven Labs (8M / 37.5%) and Lair (4M / 25.0%) are also clear avoids.

**DEF — Smoke (46r / 52.2%) is holding even, but worth monitoring.**
Smoke has been a career anchor and it's still breaking even. Worth tracking — if it dips below 45% at 50+ rounds, that's worth a more active conversation. For now, it's a reliable third option.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Flag Oregon and Villa in team ban discussions.**
Both are high-volume maps and both are below 50% WR. You play them the most — you have the clearest data on them. When the team is working out bans, push to avoid these. Border and Chalet are your clearest strengths to protect.

**2. Valkyrie/Solis is your DEF identity — keep building.**
91 rounds on Valkyrie at 62.6% and 49 rounds on Solis at 61.2%. The DEF core is confirmed. Focus continues to be ATK-side development and map selection this season.

**3. Revisit ATK op distribution.**
Jackal (35r / 45.7%) and Hibana (34r / 38.2%) are your two primary ATK ops and Hibana in particular is below baseline at significant volume. Consider whether rounds there could shift toward Chalet-style picks or operators with stronger Y11S1 results. Capitao's smaller sample has been more efficient — worth building volume there as a trial.

> **Jackal buff (Y11S1.2):** Jackal received two upgrades this patch — footprints are now visible and scannable from below through destroyed ceilings (vertical tracking), and Frag Grenades were added to his loadout. Both are direct improvements to your primary ATK op. The WR at 45.7% is already above baseline — the buff gives Jackal more tools to work with in multi-floor executes.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your Y11S1.md file._

teachingPriorities.teaching: Win% and clock are the real metrics — K/D is a byproduct
FFA warmup before every session — first-game performance delta is measurable
Fadeaway peek (shoot while retreating); ADS in cover → re-swing; quick peek sequence (info peek → ADS → crouch re-swing)
Flick first, shoot second — two separate motions; hold tighter angles than feel comfortable
Roam in sections; get the kill and leave before trading; burn the clock

bTeam.teachingPattern: Strong on winning the right fights. Primary coaching leverage: FFA warmup habit (data suggests cold-start performance dip), roaming discipline (time over kills), and cover-mechanics refinement. B Team context: ATK op restructure (drop Buck/Solid Snake, build Zero/Jackal/Thermite) is the priority work this period.

# Krafty — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Krafty | **Tracker:** Eh_KraftySmokes | **Team:** B Team

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** A developing player with strong mechanical aim and an upward rank trajectory. Lesion DEF anchor is his established identity, with ATK still taking shape.

**Tendencies:** Newest and least experienced player. Decisions and positioning are the primary gap — not aim. Plays passive utility-heavy DEF well (Lesion traps). ATK pool has been fragmented and inconsistent.

**Role fit:** DEF Anchor

**Solo vs. Group:** Benefits from group play — fewer decisions to make independently.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **High HS% + low K/D = positioning/decision problem, not aim.** The coaching priority is always "when NOT to peek" — not mechanics.
- **Lesion is the right DEF anchor.** Passive, trap-based, forces the enemy to come to him. Don't overcomplicate it.
- **ATK needs to converge on a stable identity.** Maverick has shown the best WR. Stop playing Zero (career-low K/D). Build ATK around Maverick + one breach support op.
- **Kanal** is his most reliable bad map — avoid aggressive roles here.
- **Clubhouse and Villa** are his comfort maps — use these for confidence and stack practice.
- Not ready for main-stack consideration yet. Development path: rank to Gold consistently, stabilize ATK pool, improve peak timing.

---

# Krafty — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 | 99M sample_

---

## Your Role

You're a shield-and-anchor player. Blackbeard and Montagne on ATK, Castle and Thorn on DEF — that's your identity in Y11S1, and the WR data supports it. Your K/D (0.35) is low, but your WR (49.5%) is almost even, which means the wins are coming even without the frags. That points to a utility and positioning contribution model rather than a fragging one. The coaching focus is on refining the op mix within that identity — not changing the archetype.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Castle (106r / 56.6% ✅) and Thorn (75r / 57.3% ✅) are your two DEF anchors and both are producing solid WR at very high volume. Lesion (20r / 65.0% ⭐) is your highest-WR DEF op and stands out as a meaningful signal — it's producing significantly better than your anchors in a sample that's worth taking seriously.

**Operators — ATK**
Montagne (50r / 58.0% ✅) is your clearest ATK performer — above 50% WR with a large sample. Capitao (25r / 52.0% ✅) is also positive. Blackbeard (111r / 47.7%) is just below 50% at very high volume — close to neutral, not a drag.

**Maps**
Four maps are performing well with real samples: Border (11M / 72.7% ⭐), Bank (12M / 58.3% ✅), Chalet (10M / 60.0% ✅), Clubhouse (10M / 60.0% ✅). That's a strong map base — four maps above 58% with 10–12M each. Nighthaven Labs (9M / 55.6%) is also positive and worth noting.

---

## Areas to Explore

**Operators — ATK**
Iana (43r / 34.9% ⚠️) is your third most-played ATK op and is trending noticeably below your other picks. It may be worth exploring whether those rounds could go toward Montagne or Capitao instead — both are outperforming Iana at meaningful volumes. There could be matchup or role factors driving the Iana volume, so worth thinking through before reducing it.

**Operators — DEF**
Clash (38r / 36.8% ⚠️) is your third most-played DEF op and well below your Castle/Thorn anchors. Lesion (65%/20r ⭐) is significantly outperforming it. Worth considering whether some of those Clash rounds could shift toward Lesion — the WR gap is wide.

**Maps**
Consulate (9M / 22.2%) and Oregon (8M / 25.0%) are confirmed weak spots with meaningful samples. These are worth flagging in ban discussions when your lineup has input.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Trust the shield identity — Blackbeard and Montagne are working.**
The archetype is producing. Montagne at 58% over 50 rounds is a real result. The focus should be refining the ATK pool around Blackbeard + Montagne rather than adding more variety. Capitao can be a situational third pick where ranged pressure helps.

**2. Consider giving Lesion more DEF rounds.**
65% WR over 20 rounds is the strongest DEF signal on your sheet. It may not fit every site, but on maps where trap placement is effective, Lesion could be worth trying over Clash. The WR gap between the two is significant.

**3. Protect your four strong maps.**
Border, Bank, Chalet, and Clubhouse are all 58%+ with 10-12M each. That's a genuinely strong map base. When there's any input into ban discussions, flagging those four as keeps is worth doing.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map data, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Bob — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-26 | 75M sample_

---

## Your Role

B Team DEF anchor. Your DEF pool has depth — Maestro, Thorn, and Kaid are all producing well when they get rounds. The main coaching story this season is twofold: on DEF, Nomad has built up significant volume at below-baseline WR and may be getting rounds that would be better spent elsewhere; on ATK, all primary picks are below 40% WR and finding a stable anchor is the main development priority.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Several ops are performing well in their current roles:
- Maestro — 24r | 66.7% ⭐ (your best DEF WR — Evil Eye denial is working)
- Thorn — 30r | 53.3% ✅ (consistent at meaningful volume — trap pressure converting)
- Kaid — 11r | 54.5% ✅ (small but positive sample)
- Frost — 7r | 71.4% ⭐ (small sample — strong early signal)
- Denari — 36r | 50.0% (solid second pick at meaningful volume)

Maestro at 66.7% is your best DEF op this season — worth giving it more deliberate round allocation on sites where camera denial and post-plant pressure are the priority.

**Maps — Villa is your most consistent map.**
Villa (8M / 50.0%) is your steadiest map with an even WR. Kafe Dostoyevsky (4M / 50.0%) is also even. At 75M, the map picture is still small per-map — treat these as directional signals.

---

## Areas to Explore

**DEF — Nomad has 36r at 33.3% WR.**
Nomad — 36r | 33.3% ⚠️. That's your second-highest DEF round count and it's well below baseline. Nomad's proximity alarm model works best in specific site setups — roam clearing and flank watching. If it's going in as a default pick on sites without a clear flank angle to deny, the return may not reflect the op's potential. Worth asking: on rounds where Nomad goes in, is there a specific flank lane you're denying, or is it a habit pick? If the answer is habit, Thorn or Denari may convert more rounds on those same sites.

**ATK — All primary picks are below 40% WR.**
- Brava — 34r | 32.4% ⚠️
- Thermite — 27r | 37.0% ⚠️

Both primary ATK ops are sitting below 35-37% WR at meaningful volume. The hard-breach role that Thermite fills is genuinely useful, but if the team isn't capitalizing on the breach in the execute, the personal WR suffers. Worth checking whether the execute sequence is built around the Thermite opening — if the team is breaching without a clear timed push, that could explain the result.

**Maps — Chalet and Clubhouse are both struggling this season.**
- Chalet — 7M | 28.6% ⚠️
- Clubhouse — 8M | 37.5% ⚠️

Both were stronger earlier in the season. Worth flagging both in team ban discussions when the lineup has input.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Try shifting DEF rounds from Nomad toward Maestro or Thorn.**
Nomad at 36 rounds and 33.3% WR is the clearest coaching signal right now. On sites where you'd normally default to Nomad, try Maestro (66.7%) or Thorn (53.3%) instead and track whether the result changes. Nomad can stay in the pool for specific setups with a clear flank denial purpose.

**2. Consider whether Thermite execute timing is working for the team.**
If Thermite is opening a wall but the team isn't following through with a clear timed push, the hard-breach value disappears and the personal WR reflects it. Worth a quick check with whoever calls the execute — is the Thermite breach being used as the trigger, or is it being treated as a utility pick?

**3. Denari is worth building volume on.**
36 rounds at 50.0% is a solid baseline for a newer DEF pick. Denari's utility appears to be contributing even if the WR is just at break-even. Worth continuing to develop — the DEF pool has room for it alongside Maestro and Thorn.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your Y11S1.md file._

# Hunter — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-26 | 58M sample_

---

## Your Role

B Team anchor and DEF specialist. At 58 matches you now have a reliable season picture — and the story it tells is clear: DEF is your established strength, and ATK is the area to develop. Aruni has emerged as your primary DEF op with strong results, Thorn is posting elite numbers in its role, and Castle forms a solid third. The challenge this season is that Ash at 60 rounds and 38.3% WR is your primary ATK op but trending well below baseline — developing a stronger second ATK identity is the main growth area.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — Aruni/Thorn/Castle core is established.**
- Aruni — 59r | 55.9% | 1.02 ✅ (your anchor — meaningful volume, above-baseline WR)
- Thorn — 25r | 64.0% | 1.21 ⭐ (outstanding in its rounds — best WR in your DEF pool)
- Castle — 31r | 51.6% (solid third, consistent)

The DEF core is a genuine strength. Aruni's Prisma laser grid suits a passive-hold approach well. Thorn at 64.0% over 25 rounds is a meaningful signal — it's punishing attackers in post-plant effectively. This DEF trio is working.

**ATK — Dokkaebi is your strongest performing ATK pick.**
Dokkaebi — 28r | 53.6% ✅. Above-baseline WR at real volume. The hacking pressure on enemy cameras creates disruption that appears to be converting rounds. Worth building on this as a reliable ATK secondary.

**Maps — Coastline and Kafe Dostoyevsky are your clearest strengths.**
- Coastline — 7M | 71.4% ⭐ (strong both sides)
- Kafe Dostoyevsky — 4M | 75.0% ⭐ (small sample but very positive)
- Chalet — 9M | 55.6% (above baseline at your highest-volume map)

Chalet is your most-played map and you're above 55% — that's your home ground. Coastline at 71.4% over 7 matches is a meaningful signal worth protecting in ban discussions.

---

## Areas to Explore

**ATK — Ash is your primary at 60r, but WR is at 38.3%.**
Ash — 60r | 38.3% | 0.74 ⚠️. At 60 rounds this is a real signal — not just variance. Ash has volume and it isn't converting. That could reflect site difficulty, coordination timing on entry, or a mismatch between the op's fast-entry model and how the team is using it. Worth exploring: does Ash feel different on specific sites where the run-out is clean versus sites with heavy gadget coverage? And could Dokkaebi be leading more ATK rounds as the primary, given its 53.6% WR?

**Maps — Bank (7M / 28.6%) and Nighthaven Labs (3M / 0.0%) are clear avoids.**
Bank in particular — 28.6% WR over 7 matches is a real red flag. Nighthaven is a small sample but concerning. Neither map is working for you right now. Flag both in ban discussions when you have input.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Consider giving Dokkaebi more ATK rounds as a primary trial.**
Dokkaebi (28r / 53.6%) is significantly outperforming Ash (60r / 38.3%) this season. Try leading on Dokkaebi for a session or two and track whether the ATK WR changes. The camera-hacking disruption model may suit your DEF-read style better than a straight-entry ATK approach.

> ⚠️ **Y11S2 heads-up:** Dokkaebi is receiving a remaster in Y11S2 — design intent is a refocus, not a major power shift, but her Logic Bomb mechanic may be adjusted. Continue building on her through the rest of Y11S1; revisit this coaching note at Y11S2 launch once the kit changes are known.

**2. Thorn deserves more DEF rounds.**
25 rounds at 64.0% WR is your best individual op result this season. On sites where Aruni's laser grid isn't the specific need, Thorn is worth queuing first — the Razorbloom in post-plant scenarios is producing real results. Worth growing the sample toward 40+ rounds.

**3. Flag Bank and Nighthaven at ban time.**
Your two worst-performing maps by WR. When the team has input on bans, push against these two. Chalet (55.6%) and Coastline (71.4%) are your clear strengths to protect.

---

# fEHdelCastro — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Added: 2026-05-20 | 6M sample ⚠️ SMALL SAMPLE_

---

## Note on Sample Size

Only 6 ranked matches in Y11S1 as of this brief. All observations below are grounded in career all-time data (2,370 ranked matches) rather than this season's numbers. Repull at 30M for a season-specific brief.

---

## Your Role

B Team DEF specialist. The career data is unambiguous — you're one of the strongest DEF players in the group. Thorn, Vigil, Kapkan, and Pulse all have 450+ career rounds at 57%+ WR. The challenge is ATK: no op above 49% WR with meaningful volume. That's the growth area.

---

## What's Working

**DEF pool is genuinely elite.**

Your five primary DEF ops all sit above 55% WR with massive career volume:
- Thorn — 748r | 60.4% | 1.03 ✅
- Vigil — 602r | 57.6% | 1.06 ✅
- Kapkan — 473r | 59.4% | 1.05 ✅
- Pulse — 463r | 59.0% | 1.24 ✅
- Castle — 384r | 57.0% | 0.93 ✅

Beyond those five, the depth is remarkable: Fenrir (64.7%), Maestro (63.8%), Frost (66.4%), Kaid (64.3%), Wamai (64.0%) all above 60% WR with 60+ rounds. You have options on DEF for almost any site and map.

**Map picks: Theme Park and Kafe are your clearest strengths.**
- Theme Park — 46M | 60.9% WR (DEF Win% 64.3%)
- Kafe Dostoyevsky — 60M | 58.3% WR (DEF Win% 64.2%)
- Villa — 57M | 54.4% WR

In combined games, push for these maps when you have input on bans.

---

## Areas to Explore

**ATK — no op above 49% WR at real volume.**

Glaz is your career ATK anchor at 819 rounds and 43.0% WR. That's a lot of commitment to a mechanically demanding sniper op that isn't converting rounds. Worth asking: does Glaz fit what the team needs on ATK, or is it a comfort pick from solo play?

Best candidates by career WR if you're looking to develop a better ATK option:
- Amaru — 99r | 48.5% (closest to baseline)
- Ying — 96r | 47.9%
- Deimos — 107r | 47.7%

None of these are strong, but they're better starting points than Glaz for team play.

**Maps to flag at ban time.**
- Clubhouse — 70M | 40.0% WR (your highest-volume map and your worst result — can't always avoid it but worth flagging)
- Kanal — 41M | 39.0% WR
- Chalet — 63M | 42.9% WR

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Lean into your DEF pool — it's the team's asset.**
Thorn/Vigil/Kapkan/Pulse/Castle are all proven. Pick based on site needs, not habit. The depth means you should almost never be on a sub-optimal DEF op.

**2. Explore an ATK identity beyond Glaz.**
Try giving Amaru or Ying 10–15 rounds this season and track whether your ATK WR changes. Glaz requires precise long-angle management that may be harder in 5-stack team play than in solo/duo. A site-entry-focused ATK op may suit the stack better.

**3. Flag Clubhouse in ban discussions when you have input.**
70 career matches at 40.0% WR is a real weakness. If the team can avoid it, that's a net positive for your rounds.

**4. Get to 30 matches — the real brief starts there.**
6M is too small for a reliable season picture. The priorities above are grounded in career data, not Y11S1 performance. Once you hit 30M, a proper seasonal coaching brief can be written.

---

other.profileContent:
# D-Man — Player Profile

**Callsign:** D-Man | **Tracker:** Eh_D-Man | **Team:** C Team

_Added: 2026-03-14 | ALL_TIME + Y10S4 pulled: 2026-03-14 | Previous username: TheMighty-i002 (Feb 2021)_

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** DEF anchor with strong hard-denial pool. Bandit (67.4% WR), Frost (65.1%), Azami (63.0%), Castle (61.2%), Kaid (55.4%), Kapkan (57.9%) — multiple DEF ops above 55% WR with 30+ rounds. Consistent hard-anchor identity.

**Tendencies:** Breach-utility on ATK (Buck/Thatcher/Thermite high-volume) with below-average results. The raw K/D (0.65) undersells DEF contributions — utility placements, traps, and anchor positioning generate round wins that don't show up in K/D. Zero (70r, 45.7% WR, 0.72 K/D) is the most efficient ATK op and a better choice than Buck/Thatcher.

**Role fit:** Hard-anchor DEF specialist. Mira (11r, 72.7% WR, 1.14 K/D) is a standout small-sample stat — could develop into a primary Mira main. Fenrir (6r, 1.50 K/D) also noteworthy.

**Solo vs. Group:** Insufficient recent data. 307 career ranked matches shows experience. Best season was High Calibre (Y8S4): 44M, 2,884 MMR, 0.72 K/D. Recent seasons near-absent (2M in Y10S4, 0M in Y11S1).

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Buck/Thatcher ATK is high-volume but losing** — Buck 148r at 37.2% WR, Thatcher 97r at 36.1%. These ops are losing rounds consistently. Zero (45.7%) and Thermite (51.2%) are better ATK options by WR.
- **DEF is the genuine strength.** Bandit leading at 67.4% WR (43r) is rare for a non-main-stack player. Multiple DEF ops consistently above 60% — strong anchor identity.
- **Mira is the hidden gem** — 72.7% WR, 1.14 K/D in 11r. Worth developing. If D-Man can reliably play Mira, that's a legitimate stack asset on DEF.
- **Fenrir (6r, 1.50 K/D)** — tiny sample but the highest K/D of any op with 5+ rounds. Possible fragger upside on DEF.
- First useful pull target: 10+ Y11S1 ranked matches.

---

## Season Files

| Season | File | Status |
|--------|------|--------|
| Y11S1 | Y11S1.md | 🔲 Not yet pulled — 0 ranked matches as of 2026-03-14 |
| Y10S4 | Y10S4.md | ✅ Pulled 2026-03-14 — ⚠️ 2 matches only, limited data |
| All-Time | ALL_TIME.md | ✅ Pulled 2026-03-14 |

teachingPriorities.teaching: Crosshair placement: HS% 37.8% — barricade buckle calibration, pre-aim by map knowledge
Stop over-leaning mid-shots; swing far from the wall — two mechanics fixes that require no aim practice
ATK WR is low (Buck 37.2%, Thatcher 36.2%) — hard breaching tech tree: Maverick conversion, Thermite floor angle, Ace wall sides
Move when droned (non-negotiable); stop crouch walking (walk key is faster and quieter)
Role flexibility: Flores as zero-aim fallback on bad aim days

other.teachingPattern: HS% 37.8% is the clearest priority — crosshair placement and lean discipline are the two highest-impact no-practice fixes. Hard breaching execution is the ATK WR fix. Frost/Kapkan/Azami are already performing well; operator tips are refinements on existing strength.

# TazRathmus — Player Profile

**Callsign:** TazRathmus | **Tracker:** TazRathmus | **Team:** C Team

_Added: 2026-03-14 | ALL_TIME + Y10S4 pulled: 2026-03-14 | Previous username: Eh_Tyrone (Jul 2023)_

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** DEF anchor. Rook (1,106r, 62.0% WR) is the dominant career identity — reliable anchor with consistent win rates across Mute, Mozzie, Kapkan, Kaid. DEF side is where TazRathmus earns rounds.

**Tendencies:** Hard-utility breach on ATK (Thermite/Sledge/Gridlock all high-volume), but with losing WRs across the board. The ATK side is functional, not a strength. DEF is clearly the more natural role.

**Role fit:** DEF anchor / support. Rook opener is ideal — sets up the team. Mute, Mozzie, Kapkan all show solid career WRs. Not a roamer; likely plays close and holds.

**Solo vs. Group:** Insufficient recent data to assess. Career volume (1,238 ranked) suggests experience, but recent inactivity (3 ranked Y10S4, 0 Y11S1) means current form is unknown.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **DEF anchor is the strongest contribution.** Rook/Mute/Mozzie/Kapkan/Kaid all above 54% WR with meaningful rounds — consistent, not a fluke.
- **ATK side loses rounds.** Thermite (753r, 41.4% WR), Gridlock (717r, 41.6%), Sledge (667r, 41.8%) — huge volume, below 50% WR across all three. ATK needs work or op pool needs rethinking.
- **Smoke (26r, 50.0% WR, 1.00 K/D)** is a potential upside op — better K/D than most DEF pool, has rounds. Could develop into a plant-denial closer.
- **Very low recent activity.** Peak was Deadly Omen season (298 matches, 2,886 RP). Recent seasons are near-absent. Treat current skill level as unknown until meaningful Y11S1 data exists.
- First useful pull target: 10+ Y11S1 ranked matches.

Cut Gridlock (717r, 41.6% WR) — role doesn't transfer to Thermite/Sledge identity; consolidate into breach role

other.teachingPattern: TazRathmus has enormous volume in specialized roles. Crosshair placement and Gridlock elimination are the highest-leverage changes. Kapkan and Mozzie tips are already-good mechanics with specific upgrades that compound at 300-400 rounds of volume.
